channel.title: INmaster
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INmaster, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Best Protein Sources for a Healthy, Balanced Diet
item.description: Looking to add more protein to your meals? Explore high-protein foods, including affordable options, to help meet your daily needs.

item.title: Physical and Emotional Symptoms of Depression
Weekly Meal Plan #79
item.description: I planned dinner, so you don’t have to! This weekly meal plan includes easy dinners plus a free shopping list!…

item.title: Tips to Help Keep Your Eyes Healthy
Huxe Will Give You a Personalized, Daily Audio Summary Powered by AI
item.description: The app reads your email inbox and your meeting calendar, then gives you a short audio summary. It can help you spend less time scrolling, but of cour...

item.title: ‘Love Is Blind’ Embraces the Manosphere
item.description: A show that started as an earnest if messy quest for love is now simply a mirror to the hell of modern dating.

item.title: South Korea opens the door to let Google Maps operate fully
item.description: After years of appeals, Google has finally won approval to export high-precision geographic information out of South Korea and provide proper Google M...

item.title: Sub-Zero Temperatures Didn’t Affect Madison’s Electric Bus Fleet This Year–A Major EV Milestone
item.description: In January, GNN reported on how the brain-disabling cold of the Norwegian winter couldn’t affect the performance of an all-electric ferryboat plying t...

item.title: Tin Can Is a Dumb Phone for Kids. Can Someone Teach Them How to Use It?
item.description: With its twirly cord and landline-like features, the Tin Can is giving kids a crash course in phone etiquette. For example: Talk!

item.title: Good News in History, February 27
item.description: 30 years ago today, the world of Pokémon went public, emerging from the mind of game designer Satoshi Tajiri onto Nintendo Gameboy systems. In the six...

item.title: Ultrahuman bets on redesigned smart ring to win back U.S. market after Oura dispute
item.description: Ultrahuman’s Ring Pro promises 15-day battery life and a $479 price tag as the wearables maker expands its health-tech push.

item.title: Plaid valued at $8B in employee share sale
item.description: The new valuation is a 31% increase from $6.1 billion Plaid reached in April.

item.title: Aventon Soltera 3 Electric Bike Review: A Fun Hybrid Single-Speed
item.description: Aventon’s latest single-speed hybrid bicycle is light, looks cool, and is the most bikey electric bike on the market right now.

item.title: Aktiia Hilo Blood Pressure Monitor Review: Cuffless BP Tracking
item.description: The Aktiia Hilo Blood Pressure Monitor is the first cuffless monitor to be cleared by the FDA. It’s discreet, highly accurate, and simple to use

item.title: The 10 best places to travel in April 2026
item.description: Whether you're looking for a cultural escape, a fun festival or a sunny respite from spring showers, here are some of the best places to visit in Apri...

item.title: JetBlue drops 2 more cities as it shifts to core markets
item.description: JetBlue Airways will end service to Asheville and Belize City as it focuses on more profitable flights.

item.title: Everything you need to know if you have a trip to Mexico coming up
item.description: Here's everything you should know if you have a trip to Mexico coming up, considering the cartel violence that happened this week.

item.title: Kids eat free for serious added value at these 10 luxury hotels bookable with points
item.description: A growing number of points-friendly, luxury hotels allow kids to eat for free when dining on property. These are our top picks for this money-saving h...

item.title: Who should (and shouldn’t) get the United Quest Card?
item.description: If you're on the fence about the United Quest Card, here are reasons why it does (or doesn't) make sense for you.

item.title: One-Pan Irish Chicken
item.description: This Irish chicken is my cozy mashup of pub vibes and weeknight reality. Seared chicken thighs baked over bacon, cabbage,…

item.title: Vacation Film Forgotten in 70-yo Vintage Camera Was Developed for the First Time: Do You Know These Skiers?
item.description: From the charming British countryside of Salisbury comes an equally-charming story of a roll of lost film found lodged in a thrift shop camera. Develo...

item.title: Jack Dorsey just halved the size of Block’s employee base — and he says your company is next
item.description: Jack Dorsey has long been an open admirer of Elon Musk. Now, it seems, he may have been taking notes.

item.title: Scientists Wanted Eco-Friendly Battery, Realized They Could Use the Brine Needed to Make Tofu
item.description: Capable of undergoing 120,000 charge cycles and being disposed of anywhere, an experimental new battery design might be thought of as truly state-of-t...

item.title: Beijing Travel Guide: what to do, where to stay, what to eat
item.description: We arrived in Beijing expecting chaos: traffic, horns, overwhelming noise, the stereotypical capital rush. What we found instead was a city that felt ...

item.title: Netflix backs out of bid for Warner Bros. Discovery, giving studios, HBO, and CNN to Ellison-owned Paramount
item.description: In a one-two punch of centibillion-dollar offers, the bidding war for Warner Bros. Discovery is over. David Ellison-owned Paramount will acquire Warne...

item.title: Thailand’s Mythical ‘Fire Tiger’ Captured on Trail Cam, One of the Rarest Sightings in the Cat World
item.description: Thai conservationists were delighted with the news that a sighting of an Asian golden cat had been recorded by camera traps. The legendary “fire tiger...

item.title: Crack Chicken Orzo
item.description: This crack chicken orzo bake is creamy, cheesy, and packed with that irresistible ranch, bacon, and cheddar “crack” flavor everyone…

item.title: Bang Bang Shrimp Pasta
item.description: Bang bang shrimp pasta is what weeknight dinner dreams are made of! Juicy shrimp, tender pasta, and that irresistible sweet-and-spicy…

item.title: What grocery stores reveal about how people eat
item.description: A grocery store is one of the most revealing places you can walk into when travelling. More than restaurants or cafés, it shows how people actually ea...

item.title: What is considered a “normal meal” in different cultures

item.title: 63 Essential Things to Know Before Visiting Tokyo

item.title: Jobless claims fall to lowest level since mid-May
Jobless claims stay low in latest week
Consumer credit growth soars in December
U.S. productivity slows down in fourth quarter while unit labor costs accelerate
Beyond to buy rights to Buy Buy Baby brand and reunite it with Bed Bath & Beyond
